title: T-050 — Warehouse stock movement and adjustment UI surfaces
prompt:
# T-050 — Warehouse stock movement and adjustment UI surfaces

PRD: `05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md`.
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Implement stock movements list, movement modal, manager approvals, and cycle-count quick adjustment P1 stub with prototype parity.

## Implementation contract
Build stock_movement_list_page, stock_move_modal, and cycle_count_quick_adjustment_modal. Include partial-move split notice, >10% threshold approval state, reason-code validation, side-panel detail, and P1 stub messaging for full cycle counts.

## Files (scope)
- apps/web/app/(app)/warehouse/movements/** [create]
- apps/web/components/warehouse/movements/** [create]
- design/Monopilot Design System/warehouse/movement-screens.jsx
- design/Monopilot Design System/warehouse/modals.jsx

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given movement list renders, when compared to design/Monopilot Design System/warehouse/movement-screens.jsx label stock_movement_list_page, then filters, tabs, side-panel, quantities, and approval warnings match.
2. Given stock_move_modal receives qty less than LP qty, then it shows the partial split cascade notice and blocks invalid qty greater than LP qty.
3. Given cycle_count_quick_adjustment_modal delta is greater than 10%, then footer action changes to Submit for Approval and reason_code remains mandatory.
4. Given components are inspected, then data-prototype-label markers exist for stock_movement_list_page, stock_move_modal, and cycle_count_quick_adjustment_modal.

## Test strategy
- RED: component tests for movement modal validation and delta threshold behavior.
- GREEN: Playwright test side-panel open/close and approval tab flows.
- Run pnpm test and pnpm playwright test --grep @warehouse-movement-ui.

## Risk red lines
- Do not treat full cycle counts as P1.
- Do not allow adjustment without reason.
- Do not move blocked LPs through this UI.

cross_module_dependencies.contract: rule_registry, warehouses/locations/printers, feature flags, tenant security policy
PO/TO/WO read models, RM root reservations, WO release/cancel hooks
LP lookup, barcode validation, lock protocol, FEFO suggestion, consume-to-WO
WO material consumption and output LP creation events
